title: toxicodendron incanum
intro: Toxicodendron incanum, also known as poison oak, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its toxic sap that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in humans. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Toxicodendron incanum include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever and pain relief. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used topically or taken internally, but caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential allergic reactions.
